analysisHub.title: NL East Race
analysisHub.body: As the MLB season cruises by, the imposters start falling back, and the contenders start coming alive. One of the tightest pennant races right now is the NL East. While it seemed the Braves had an easy season ahead, the rest of the East had other plans. The Braves started the season hot taking a 10.5 game lead early on and the Phillies 10 games under .500 at 9-19. That was, until Don Mattingly took over as interim manager. Since Mattingly took over, the Phils are 41-20 and now only 2.5 games behind the struggling Braves who are 3-7 in their last 10 games due to offensive slumps, pitching struggles, and an injury to their superstar, Ronald Acuna. Brandon Marsh has emerged as a solid piece in the lineup hitting .305 with 46 RBI's. Schwarber has been mashing the baseball with 31 HR's, on pace for the record. The rest of the Phillies lineup has improved, showing they are a team to watch out for. Will Cy Young candidate Cristopher Sanchez continue his performance? Will the rest of the rotation and bullpen hold their own? We'll see. But the East isn't just between Atlanta and Philly, Miami is just 3 games behind Atlanta, and 0.5 behind the Phils. With the 2nd cheapest payroll, only above Cleveland, Miami has shocked the league with their performance so far. With the help of their pitching staff and emergence of a young core, Sandy Alcantara is leading the league in Quality Starts, and a 10-4 record paired with Max Meyer with a 2.58 ERA, 7th best in the league. Otto Lopez has had a standout year so far leading the league in batting average with a .343 AVG with 124 hits, Xavier Edwards also hitting .300, and Liam Hicks 13 HR and 56 RBI's. Will they still be in the race for the pennant come September? In 4th place we have the Washington Nationals. With the most runs scored in the entire league with 500, you'd think they have a better record than 47-46? Wrong. They have also given up the 3rd most runs in the league with 493. Just behind the A's at 494 and the Rockies at 530. Their offense has been explosive with young talent like James Wood hitting 25 HR, 61 RBI's, and a .949 OPS along with CJ Abrams and Luis Garcia Jr. both with 19 HR and 65 RBI's each. People are questioning if the young manager should put Wood further down in the lineup to produce even more RBI's instead of just hitting solo home runs in the leadoff spot. There are no other words for the Nat's pitching staff other than abysmal. They have the worst bullpen in the league by far with absolutely no talent. Foster Griffin has the best ERA of the starts with a 2.87 which is solid, but the next highest starter is Cade Cavalli with a 3.88 ERA. Just terrible. I'm not even going to waste my time writing about the Mets as they have been out of the race since opening day. They're 15 games behind with no clue what they're doing in the dugout. They just fired Mendoza, but it took them way too long, and they have no leader in the dugout since Soto got paid. Just pitiful. Highest payroll and not going to make playoffs 2 consecutive years. I'd hate to be selling tickets for that team... So let's see how the NL East turns out, will the Braves turn it around? Can the Phillies keep the hot streak? Will the Marlins continue producing? Will the Nats make moves for pitching by the deadline? Will the Mets discontinue the franchise? Stay tuned.

todayInSports.title: Kid Nichols Joins the 300-Win Club
todayInSports.author: Gary
todayInSports.body:
July 7th, 1900 - Charles "Kid" Nichols of the Boston Beaneaters defeats the Chicago Orphans 11-4 to become the youngest player in MLB History to join the 300-win club.

Initially, I selected this fact as today's History Bulletin solely because of the names of the two competing teams. Both names feel like something you can only find in the weird and wacky early eras of Major League Baseball. The Orphans would only be the nickname for Chicago for four years, 1898-1902, until changing the epithet to the Cubs in 1903. Boston would go on to have several different titles, including the Boston Bees, until landing on the Braves in the 40s. The team would then go on to relocate from Boston to Milwaukee and then to their current home in Atlanta.

However, as fun as classic MLB team names are, what truly caught my eye in my research was the career of Charles "Kid" Nichols. Making his MLB debut in 1890, he would go on to rack up 10 consecutive seasons with 20 or more wins and 11 in total over his 16-year career. 7 of these seasons would also cross over the 30-win mark as well. The best season of his career came in 1893, when he was truly dealing - with an overall record of 34-14, 43 of which were complete games, and racked up a total of 425 innings pitched.

If that is not enough evidence to tip your cap to Mr. Nichols, just know he was a two-sport athlete - winning the Kansas City Class A Bowling Championship at the age of 68!

baseball.author: Lucas
baseball.title: Today's Board:
baseball.body:
LEAVING THX
Jackson Chourio (MIL) vs. STL (RHP Matt Svanson)
Odds: +500 to homer
Chourio's white hot - .292 average, .524 slug, 13 homers in 55 games (deep in 20% of his games this year). Today he draws Matt Svanson, a 2-1 arm with a shaky 4.67 ERA - not exactly lockdown stuff. Only wrinkle: Chourio went 0-for-4 last time he saw the Cardinals. But a shaky ERA arm plus a red-hot bat says the yard's calling his name.

MORTGAGE THE HOUSE
Luis Garcia Jr. (WSH) vs. HOU (RHP Tatsuya Imai)
Odds: +540 to homer
Garcia's an absolute menace right now - 18-19 homers on the year, carrying the Nationals offense outright with 9 homers and a 1.394 OPS over his last 15 games. Imai is turrible.

100% VIBES
Colson Montgomery (CWS) vs. BOS
Odds: +330 to homer
No overthinking this one - Montgomery's in the middle of a legit power surge, 23 homers already, going deep in nearly 26% of his games this year. "Big Smooth" is just seeing it right now. Pure gut call.

LOTTO TICKET
Vladimir Guerrero Jr. (TOR) vs. SF
Odds: +750 to homer
He's Vlad Guerrero. He should consider hitting a home run.

SICKO PICK
Jordan Walker (STL) vs. MIL (RHP Jacob Misiorowski)
Odds: +560 to homer
Misiorowski is filthy - 1.47 ERA, 0.78 WHIP, hit 104.5 mph this year (an MLB record for a starter). Walker's the real deal too: .292 average, 20 bombs, top-20 OPS in the league. Betting a dinger off one of the nastiest arms in baseball isn't smart, it's sick. That's the point.

RETIREMENT LAY (buy a log cabin in Montana and never be heard from again)
Parlay: Luis Garcia Jr. (+540) + Colson Montgomery (+330) + Jackson Chourio (+500)
Combined odds: ~+16,412
Three of the hottest bats on the slate, stacked into one. Hit all three and it's not a bet, it's a life event - pack the truck, Montana's calling.

soccer.author: Liam
soccer.title: Semi-Finals Preview
soccer.body: WOW WOW WOW What a round of 16 we had close games, goals galore, defensive battles we had it all. Now we are onto the next round the quarter finals 8 teams remain to become champions of the world. Games are not until later in the week, but I am just gonna give a brief overview of each game as I see it today.  France V Morocco - fantastic game here a rematch of the 2022 semifinal. Morocco are going out for revenge I do believe they will put out a defensive strategy for this game and force France to have a moment of brilliance. France should win as they are the most talented team remaining in my opinion but don’t count out Morocco as they will look to strike early and defend.  Spain V Belgium - This should be quick Spain by a million thx. No but seriously don’t be fooled by Belgium last game they just don’t have it man Spain is better in all phases of the game #401K on Spain. Lamine, Pedri, Rodri are going to run and pass around this Belgium like they are a U9 boys team. I would be shocked if Belgium pull this off but I guess you never know with this game especially in the World Cup.  Norway V England - wow this game will be fantastic Norway have looked great and Haaland unstoppable. England are the better team on paper but have looked bad in points of this tournament and you can’t do that against this team they will capitalize and make you pay for it. Norway will have less of the ball but as soon as they get they will look to get to in my opinion the player of the tournament Erling Haaland. This will be a great game and I can not wait! Argentina V Switzerland - I’m sorry I don’t see Argentina losing yes they were on the verge of losing to Egypt but they just have this IT FACTOR that I can’t explain. The Swiss will look to make this 0-0 and go to pens I just don’t think have the attacking power to make this game close. As well Messi won’t go out losing to Switzerland I’m sorry he won’t. Argentina will prevail and advance to yet another semi-final.   My predictions, in depth analysis, and bets will come out the day of the games stay tuned.

soccer.author: Liam
soccer.title: Switzerland vs Colombia - Match Prediction
soccer.body:
The next and final game for today is Switzerland v Colombia. Unlike the other game, I think this will be a close battle. The Swiss have a very strong side led by their captain for many years, Granit Xhaka. I believe the Swiss will have many chances but not have much of the ball in this game - the Colombian side just has too many attacking weapons and are flat out more talented, with Luis Diaz having a fantastic tournament so far. This will be a close battle and a great watch if you love defensive battles with a moment of brilliance. In this case I believe Colombia will provide that moment.

Prediction
Colombia 1
Switzerland 0

Bets
Safe bets: Colombia to advance, Under 3.5 goals

Risky bets: Over 2.5 goals, Draw 90 minutes

Hot bets: Diaz anytime goal scorer, Embolo 2+ shots on target

opinions.author: Cade
opinions.title: USMNT Embarasses America
opinions.body:
You can't win at everything.

It's a simple fact of life. No civilization to exist in recorded human history has been able to accomplish such a feat. Last night, America had a come to Jesus realization. We fucking suck at soccer.

We were dooped again in the every 4 year World Cup soccer fandom cycle. Maybe it's just on us at this point. How could we be so stupid as to believe that wins against Paraguay, Australia, and Boznia-Herzosomething were a true indicator of how good this band of Z-tier American athletes and castaways from foreign countries were?

The "men" that represented us last night should simply be ashamed. As a red blooded American Patriot, i think a fair compromise would be for Christian Pulisic to immediately relinquish citizenship to the United States of America. I don't think he should ever be allowed to step foot in the 48 contiguous ever again (potentially open to him owning property in Alaska or Hawaii should he agree). I read somewhere that he has Croatian citizenship as well. Christian, America would love for you to switch and represent them.

The American soccer propaganda machine was up to their usual tricks after last nights curb stomping. "They won over the hearts of Americans" or "The future is bright, we just aren't there yet". We will never get there.

It's just that simple. We don't care about that sport in this country. Think anyone in Belgium can hit a 100mph fastball? Or bring down Derrick Henry as he breaks into the second level? The answer is absolutely fucking not. Kids in Belgium grow up stuffing their fucking faces with waffles, chocolates and dreaming of the World Cup. We want to score the winning touchdown in the Super Bowl.

If the President is going to create an International incident so that a Nigerian-Brit can play for the U.S. in a soccer game, you better be able to back it up. Last night is a clear indication that America will never be able to back it up. Come 2030 let's save everyone some time. Don't bother putting a team on the field for a sport we don't care about.
